brief_justification: Agent correctly identifies 'employment unchanged' as the critical unstated assumption with valid derivation from the arithmetic; agent traces second-order employer responses (hours cuts, layoffs, automation) and explains why the assumption is load-bearing versus others; agent notes 'amplify/suppress' signals and distinguishes this assumption from alternatives but does not explicitly reflect on its own reasoning biases mid-analysis; agent verifies by showing the mathematical breakdown when hours change, though stops short of boundary-condition testing; agent acknowledges 'other assumptions exist' (price effects) but does not provide structured confidence levels or flag what would flip the conclusion; agent explicitly names the price-level alternative and explains why it is less critical than the employment assumption; premises P1–P4 → C are laid out in a table with the hidden premise precisely located, fully reproducible; agent avoids accepting the argument's premises uncritically and reconstructs from the arithmetic up, but does not explicitly name the negative gate pattern; neither suppressed failure mode (inherited belief acceptance or stacking unverified assumptions) appears in the response; agent follows decompose→test→flag topology but does not explicitly execute the axiom/derived gate or the saturation-exit MOD shown in the topology.
